summary: All 30 criteria pass. The test file meta-tests/test-utils-pure.bats is well-structured, covers all 5 pure functions (slugify, sprint_pad, sprint_dir, json_read, file_exists) with comprehensive edge cases, and all 26 functional tests plus 2 structural, 1 integration, and 1 quality criterion are satisfied. Sprint 1 regression (30 criteria) also passes cleanly with zero modifications to sprint-1 files.

criteriaResults.evidence: File meta-tests/test-utils-pure.bats exists. Line 5 contains: load 'helpers/test-helper'.
setup() function calls source_harness_lib 'utils.sh' at line 16, before any @test blocks execute.
Test 'slugify: converts uppercase to lowercase' passes (ok 9). Asserts slugify 'HELLO' == 'hello'.
Test 'slugify: replaces special characters with hyphens' passes (ok 10). Asserts slugify 'hello world' == 'hello-world'.
Test 'slugify: collapses consecutive special characters into a single hyphen' passes (ok 11). Asserts slugify 'hello---world' == 'hello-world'.
Test 'slugify: strips leading and trailing hyphens' passes (ok 12). Asserts slugify '---hello---' == 'hello'.
Test 'slugify: truncates output to 50 characters' passes (ok 13). Provides long input, asserts ${#output} -le 50.
Test 'slugify: handles empty string input' passes (ok 14). Asserts slugify '' == ''.
Test 'slugify: passes through already-slugified input unchanged' passes (ok 15). Asserts slugify 'already-slugified' == 'already-slugified'.
Test 'slugify: handles spaces and tabs correctly' passes (ok 16). Uses $'hello\tworld here', asserts output == 'hello-world-here'.
Test 'slugify: handles mixed case with numbers' passes (ok 17). Asserts slugify 'Build V2 App' == 'build-v2-app'.
Test 'sprint_pad: pads single digit to two digits with leading zero' passes (ok 18). Asserts sprint_pad 3 == '03'.
Test 'sprint_pad: preserves double-digit numbers as-is' passes (ok 19). Asserts sprint_pad 12 == '12'.
Test 'sprint_pad: pads zero to 00' passes (ok 20). Asserts sprint_pad 0 == '00'.
Test 'sprint_pad: handles large numbers without truncation' passes (ok 21). Asserts sprint_pad 100 == '100'.
Test 'sprint_dir: constructs correct path for single-digit sprint number' passes (ok 22). Asserts sprint_dir 3 == 'harness-state/sprints/sprint-03'.
Test 'sprint_dir: constructs correct path for double-digit sprint number' passes (ok 23). Asserts sprint_dir 12 == 'harness-state/sprints/sprint-12'.
Test 'sprint_dir: respects HARNESS_STATE environment variable override' passes (ok 24). Sets HARNESS_STATE='custom-state', asserts sprint_dir 5 == 'custom-state/sprints/sprint-05'.
Test 'json_read: reads a top-level field from a JSON file' passes (ok 25). Creates JSON with .name field, asserts json_read returns 'test-project'.
Test 'json_read: reads a nested field using dot-path notation' passes (ok 26). Reads .git.branch from nested JSON, asserts 'main'.
Test 'json_read: reads an array element' passes (ok 27). Reads .sprints[0].name, asserts 'first'.
Test 'json_read: returns empty string for missing file' passes (ok 28). Calls json_read on nonexistent file, asserts status 0 and output == ''.
Test 'json_read: returns empty or null for missing field in valid JSON' passes (ok 29). Reads .nonexistent, accepts 'null' or ''.
Test 'json_read: handles malformed JSON gracefully without crashing' passes (ok 30). Feeds '{invalid json content}', asserts status 0 and empty output.
Test 'file_exists: returns true for a non-empty file' passes (ok 31). Creates file with content, asserts exit code 0.
Test 'file_exists: returns false for a missing file' passes (ok 32). Asserts exit code non-zero for nonexistent file.
Test 'file_exists: returns false for an empty file' passes (ok 33). Creates empty file via touch, asserts exit code non-zero.
Test 'file_exists: returns false for a directory path' passes (ok 34). Passes directory to file_exists, asserts exit code non-zero.
bash meta-tests/run.sh exits with code 0. All 34 tests pass (8 from sprint-1 infrastructure + 26 from sprint-2 pure function tests). Output: '1..34' with all 'ok'. Completed in 1s.

All 26 @test names in test-utils-pure.bats follow 'function_name: description of behavior' pattern. Examples: 'slugify: converts uppercase to lowercase', 'sprint_pad: pads single digit to two digits with leading zero', 'json_read: reads a top-level field from a JSON file', 'file_exists: returns true for a non-empty file'.

sprint1.details: All sprint-1 deliverables intact. Directory structure verified (C1-01). run.sh executable with bats/jq prerequisite checks and --filter support (C1-02..C1-07). test-helper.bash functions verified: setup/teardown, init_test_repo, source_harness_lib, install_fixture, assert_json_field, run_hook (C1-08..C1-16). All 20 fixture files present and valid JSON (C1-17..C1-27). meta-tests/ independent from tests/ - zero cross-references (C1-28). Full suite runs cleanly (C1-29). All fixture JSON parses without errors (C1-30). No sprint-1 files were modified.

correctness.rationale: All 30 criteria pass. Every test correctly exercises the function under test with appropriate assertions on both exit status and output. Tests properly use bats 'run' keyword to capture output and exit codes.

completeness.rationale: Every function specified in the contract (slugify, sprint_pad, sprint_dir, json_read, file_exists) is covered with the required number of test cases. No criterion was missed or partially implemented.

codeQuality.rationale: Clean, well-structured bats test file with clear section separators. Setup function correctly sources the library under test. Test names are descriptive and follow a consistent naming convention. Minor observation: the setup() in test-utils-pure.bats duplicates logic from test-helper.bash's setup() rather than calling it and extending, but this is a valid pattern in bats and avoids coupling.

testQuality.rationale: Tests are well-isolated (each gets a fresh temp dir via mktemp), assertions are clear and specific, edge cases are covered (empty strings, missing files, malformed JSON, large numbers, directory paths). The json_read missing-field test correctly accepts both 'null' and '' to handle jq -r behavior variations.

regressionSafety.rationale: Zero sprint-1 files modified. All 8 sprint-1 tests continue to pass. The new test file integrates cleanly via the existing run.sh discovery mechanism with no configuration changes needed.

notes: Excellent implementation. All 30 criteria pass on first attempt. The test file is clean, well-organized, and follows bats best practices. Sprint-1 regression tests all pass with zero modifications to existing files. The new test file was automatically discovered by run.sh via its find-based .bats discovery.
